title: Project Report: PDF Q&A Chatbot with RAG — Generative AI Engineer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Upload PDFs, chunk and embed with OpenAI/HuggingFace, answer questions with retrieved context.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**PDF Q&A Chatbot with RAG** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Generative AI Engineer. Duration: 2–3 weeks. Upload PDFs, chunk and embed with OpenAI/HuggingFace, answer questions with retrieved context. Tools: LangChain, OpenAI, Chroma, Streamlit.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: PDF text extraction and chunking
Objective: Vector store (Chroma/FAISS)
Objective: RAG prompt pipeline
Objective: Simple Streamlit or Gradio UI
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Generative AI Engineer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Generative AI Engineer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Generative AI Engineer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Ingest
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Ingest** *(Level: Beginner)*

PDF → chunks. Complete these tasks: LangChain loader; Splitter config. Deliverable for this phase: Indexed documents.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] LangChain loader
- [ ] Splitter config
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Ingest is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Generative AI Engineer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Indexed documents to the teacher.


chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: GitHub repo; Demo video; Sample Q&A log. Skills demonstrated: RAG, Embeddings, LLM APIs. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: GitHub repo
Deliverable: Demo video
Deliverable: Sample Q&A log
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits GitHub repo and passes the rubric.
key_takeaways: Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.
